_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Problem with the rate or rhythm of the heartbeat (too fast/tachycardia, too slow/bradycardia, or irregular rhythm)
Symptoms such as irregular heartbeat, dizziness, or difficulty breathing

Underlying conditions including Bradyarrhythmia, Premature or extra heart beat, Supraventricular arrhythmia (atrial fibrillation, atrial flutter, paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia), or Ventricular arrhythmia (ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation)

preauth.label: Clinical notes detailing history and Admission notes showing vitals and examination findings.
Investigation reports establishing diagnosis ECG/ECHO/Cardiac Enzymes/ Angiography/ Holter?

claim.label: Relevant Investigations report ECG/ECHO/Cardiac Enzymes/ Angiography/ Holter

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailing history and Admission notes showing vitals and examination findings.

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?

common_queries: Patient's ECG/ECHO/Cardiac enzymes/Angiography/Holter reports are not suggestive of the diagnosis.
Clinical notes detailing history and admission notes showing vitals and examination findings are missing or incomplete.
Investigation reports establishing diagnosis (ECG/ECHO/Cardiac Enzymes/Angiography/Holter) are missing at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) are missing at claim submission.
Detailed discharge summary is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: An arrhythmia is a problem with the rate or rhythm of the heartbeat (too fast/tachycardia, too slow/bradycardia, or irregular rhythm).
Causes include changes in heart tissue/activity or electrical signals due to damage from disease, injury, or genetics.
Symptoms may include irregular heartbeat, dizziness, or difficulty breathing.

Types include Bradyarrhythmia/Bradycardia, Premature/extra heart beat, Supraventricular arrhythmia (atrial fibrillation, atrial flutter, paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia), and Ventricular arrhythmia (ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation).

Management involves Healthy Lifestyle Changes, Medicines (Adenosine, Atropine, Beta Blockers, calcium channel Blockers, Digitalis/Digoxin, Sodium Channel Blockers, Potassium Channel Blockers), and Procedures (Cardioversion, Catheter ablation, Implantable cardioverter defibrillators (ICDs), Pacemakers) if medicines are ineffective.
